mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at the zero lower bound signals sustained monetary accommodation, compressing discount rates and lifting equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for any 3-month period and the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than half of those months, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% reflects extreme labor market slack that historically precedes a sustained recovery in hiring and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 9.5% but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such episodes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales signal that both consumer and investment demand are gaining traction, jointly driving above-trend equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both rising) is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than one-third of cases, the interaction effect is not valid.

mechanism_narrative: During deflationary regimes, a rise in CPI signals that deflation fears are receding and demand is firming, which supports equity prices as it reduces real debt burdens and boost consumer spending.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises any month while year-over-year inflation is negative, but the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than half of such cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll employment level above 130,000 (in thousands) indicates that the labor market is adding jobs at a pace consistent with a durable recovery, quickly lifting investor sentiment and stock prices within a week.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS exceeds 130,000 but the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in more than 35% of such instances, the signal is unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low interest rates combined with rising retail sales indicate that monetary stimulus is transmitting to consumer demand, a powerful joint signal for positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in at least 40% of occurrences, the interactive effect is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at extremely depressed levels (below 600,000) represent a floor for housing activity; historically such lows imply that the subsequent recovery in housing will be strong, supporting equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST stays below 600,000 and the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative more often than positive, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production from a very low base signals that manufacturing is emerging from recession, boosting cyclical equities within a week.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises month-over-month but the S&P 500 5-day return is negative in more than 45% of cases, the signal is not reliable.
